hero.hero_strength: Strong early game damage. High burst from skills. Good mobility with skill 2. Excellent at poking and finishing enemies. Safe long range ultimate.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Granger synergizes well with heroes that provide strong crowd control, initiation, and frontline protection. Tanks and supports that can lock enemies in place allow Granger to safely deal burst damage from the backline. This synergy helps Granger land his skills and Ultimate more effectively during team fights.

counters.counter_desc: Khufra is a hard counter to Granger due to his ability to disable mobility and apply heavy crowd control. His Bouncing Ball skill prevents Granger from using his dash effectively, while his crowd control makes it easy to lock Granger down in team fights. Granger struggles to reposition and deal damage when pressured by Khufra.

dominants.dominant_desc: Maximize farm efficiency and apply constant pressure using skill-based poke. Maintain optimal positioning, track enemy cooldowns, and rotate early for objectives. Capitalize on Granger burst damage to secure picks and snowball the game.

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose this emblem because it suits Granger skill-based burst playstyle. The Marksman Emblem increases his poke and damage, while Impure Rage adds extra adaptive damage and helps him use his skills more often, making him stronger in early to mid game fights.

equips.equip_desc: This build focuses on reducing cooldowns and increasing skill spam potential. Hunter Strike and Sky Piercer provide cooldown reduction and burst damage, allowing Granger to use his skills more frequently. Malefic Roar helps penetrate tanky enemies, while Sea Halberd reduces enemy regeneration. Rose Gold Meteor gives extra survivability with its shield, making this build effective in extended fights and constant poking.

This build is designed to shred tanky heroes and frontliners. Malefic Roar and Blade of Despair provide high physical penetration and damage, allowing Granger to deal massive burst even against high-defense enemies. Endless Battle adds true damage and sustain, making him effective in prolonged fights. Tough Boots improve durability against crowd control.

This build maximizes Granger burst damage to quickly eliminate squishy heroes. Blade of the Heptaseas and Sky Piercer enhance early game pressure and skill burst. Endless Battle adds true damage after skills, while Blade of Despair greatly increases overall damage output. This build is best for fast kills and snowballing the game.

hero.hero_overview: Granger is a Marksman hero known for his high burst damage and skill-based playstyle. He does not rely on attack speed but instead focuses on proper skill timing and accuracy. Granger is commonly played in the Gold Lane, where he can farm efficiently and pressure enemies. In team fights, he excels as a backline damage dealer and a finisher, especially with his ultimate skill, Death Sonata.
hero.hero_strength:
High burst damage, especially in the early to mid game

Strong at pick-offs and finishing low HP enemies

Not dependent on attack speed items

Effective at long-range poke damage

Can carry games with good positioning and skill usage
hero.hero_weakness:
Vulnerable to crowd control and assassin burst

No escape skill, making him easy to gank

Requires good aim and timing

Weaker in the late game if behind in farm

Highly dependent on proper positioning during team fights

data.recommend: This guide provides effective strategies for playing Granger, including his best team synergies and the strongest counters to watch out for. Whether you're a new or experienced player, these tips will

spell.spell_desc:
Flicker is the best battle spell for Granger because he has no built-in escape skill. It helps him quickly reposition during fights, escape from ganks, or chase enemies when needed. Flicker is especially useful for maintaining proper positioning in team fights and avoiding crowd control from assassins and divers.

Using Flicker offensively allows Granger to surprise enemies and secure kills with his skills or ultimate. Defensively, it provides a reliable way to survive dangerous situations and reset positioning after casting Death Sonata.

spell.spell_desc: Now days with all off the Chain CC Meta Purify is probably the best spell for heroes without durability or anti CC Skills/Passives. If the enemy team doesn't have heavy CCs or too many quick CCs you can go for Flicker.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: The fatal flaw of the Granger and Estes pairing is their brittle inflexibility when faced with isolation or specialized suppression. Because their success depends entirely on "clumping" together, they are highly susceptible to "crowd control" or "area-of-effect" disruptions that can paralyze both at once, effectively putting all their eggs in one basket. In a work context, if the supporter (Estes) is targeted first or neutralized by "anti-heal" tactics—like high-stress environments that outpace their ability to help—the high-output worker (Granger) is left completely exposed with no natural defense or stamina. This creates a single point of failure: if you separate them or cut off the support line, the duo's synergy vanishes, leaving the worker to crumble under pressure and the supporter useless without a lead to protect.

The compatibility between Granger and Carmilla centers on turning single-target burst into massive area damage. Carmilla uses her ultimate to link multiple enemies together so that they share a portion of the damage and crowd control dealt to any one of them. This creates a perfect window for Granger, whose high-damage bullets and explosive ultimate usually hit only one target at a time. When he fires into a group linked by Carmilla, his concentrated firepower is distributed across all connected enemies, allowing him to wipe out an entire team simultaneously. Their success depends on precise timing, as Granger must unleash his full damage while the enemies are bound together before they can move apart to break the link.

counters.counter_desc: Granger counters Beatrix because he has better movement and range control. Beatrix is most dangerous when she is very close or very far, but she often has to slow down to aim her most powerful shots. Granger uses his dash to stay at a medium distance where her weapons are less accurate, and he uses his bullets to deal damage while constantly moving. Because Granger can shoot while running, he can easily dodge her attacks and burst her down before she can switch to the right weapon to respond.

Granger counters Ixia because he is fast and she is still. When Ixia uses her ultimate, she cannot move, making her a "sitting duck." Granger uses his S2 (Dash) to stay outside her fan-shaped attack zone while using his S1 (Bullets) and Ult (Snipe) to hit her from a distance. Since Granger can move while shooting, he "out-kites" Ixia, dodging her skills while delivering heavy burst damage that she cannot escape.

dominants.dominant_desc: During Grangers dominant phase positioning is very important He should stay at the edge of fights and use his long range skills to safely deal damage to squishy enemies like marksmen and mages He can maximize damage by using Rhapsody skill one with Death Sonata ultimate while Rondo skill two helps him dodge crowd control and reposition Granger should keep moving between shots and use slow or debuff items to control enemies Using Flicker or Purify at the right time can save him and keeping energy stacks high ensures his skills are ready When fights are risky he can split push to pressure turrets and make the most of his range and damage potential

emblems.emblem_desc: Using the Custom Assassin Emblem on Granger maximizes his burst damage, sustain in fights, and finishing potential. Rupture increases his physical penetration, allowing his skills and bullets to deal more damage to enemies with armor, making his burst much more effective, especially in mid to late game. Festival of Blood provides lifesteal whenever he deals damage, helping him sustain during skirmishes and recover HP after poking or using his skills. Finally, Lethal Ignition enhances his kill potential by dealing extra burn damage over time to enemies he hits, making it easier for Granger to finish off low-health targets and pressure opponents in team fights.

With this Custom Assassin Emblem setup, Granger becomes more effective in prolonged skirmishes and team fights while maintaining strong burst potential. Thrill increases his damage when he is low on HP, allowing him to deal even more lethal burst in clutch situations instead of falling off. Festival of Blood provides spell vamp, helping Granger sustain himself by recovering HP whenever he deals damage with his skills, which is especially useful since most of his damage comes from ability-based shots. Impure Rage boosts his damage against enemies with higher HP than him, making it easier for Granger to shred tankier targets and win duels, while also improving his overall impact in team fights.

For survivability, this Custom Fighter Emblem setup provides a balanced mix of durability and damage, allowing Granger to stay longer in fights while still dealing effective burst. With Rupture granting high adaptive penetration and adaptive attack, his damage remains strong even against tankier enemies, making him both resilient and threatening in team engagements.
emblems.emblem_title: High Spell Vamp with High Damage

equips.equip_desc: Grangers build focuses on high burst damage, penetration, and survivability. Skypiercer increases attack and movement speed after using skills. Tough Boots reduce crowd control and add magic resistance. Blade of Heptases and Malefic Roar boost damage against tanky enemies, while Hunter Strike improves skill cooldowns and mobility. Blade of Despair increases attack and finishing power. Alternative items include Immortality and Winter Crown, and Malefic Roar can be replaced with Rose Gold Meteor for more survivability.

hero.hero_overview: Granger is a burst-type Marksman in Mobile Legends known for his long-range poke and deadly skill damage. He relies on Demonic Energy instead of mana, allowing him to spam abilities as he gets takedowns. Granger is weaker in the early game but becomes extremely powerful once he gets his core items, making him a strong carry in mid to late game. He excels at deleting squishy heroes from a distance with his skills while using his dash to reposition and stay safe in fights.
hero.hero_strength:
Granger — Hero Strengths
High Burst Damage — Can quickly delete squishy heroes with his skills.
Long-Range Poke — Strong at dealing damage safely from a distance.
Great Snowball Potential — Becomes very powerful once ahead in farm or kills.
Good Mobility — His dash helps him reposition or escape danger.
Strong Finisher — His ultimate is excellent for securing kills in team fights.
hero.hero_weakness:
Granger — Hero Weaknesses
Weak Early Game — Needs items and farm before becoming strong.
Very Squishy — Easily killed by assassins and burst heroes.
Crowd Control (CC) Vulnerability — Struggles when stunned, slowed, or immobilized.
Skill-Dependent — Missed shots greatly reduce his damage output.
Positioning Reliant — One mistake in placement can get him instantly deleted.

data.recommend: I recommend this guide for players who want to master the art of "hit and run" tactics. Granger consider as the God of Dodge Hero.

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is a highly useful battle spell for Granger as it provides instant repositioning, allowing him to escape dangerous situations, dodge crowd control, or aggressively close the gap to finish off low-health enemies. It also helps him secure better angles for his skills, especially his ultimate, making his plays more flexible and unpredictable in team fights. However, if the enemy team has strong burst damage or multiple crowd control heroes that can easily lock Granger down and kill him, Purify can be used as a reliable alternative to remove debuffs and increase his survivability in critical moments.

Crowd control ultimate traps Granger, making him an easy target.
Masha counters Granger because she can dive onto him, absorb his burst with high durability, and force him to retreat, making it hard for him to farm or deal damage safely.

dominants.dominant_desc: Granger Dominant Phase is important because in this stage, he should focus on farming minions in the Gold Lane to stack energy and build items, which lets him use his skills more often, deal higher burst damage, and safely go for kills on low-HP or out-of-position enemies.
dominants.dominant_title: Farm, Passive Stacking , Go for Kill

emblems.emblem_desc:
Granger is a burst marksman, so Rupture Emblem amplifies his skill combos and finishing potential, letting beginners secure kills more reliably without relying solely on items.

Weapon Master increases all of Granger Physical Attack and Magic Power by 5% making every item, talent, and skill hit stronger, giving beginners and experienced players a noticeable damage boost.


Impure Rage gives Granger bonus damage and small healing, helping him burst enemies and survive a bit longer, which is perfect for his skill-based playstyle.

equips.equip_desc: This build is strong for Granger because it provides high physical damage and armor/magic penetration, allowing him to burst squishy targets and shred tanky enemies efficiently.

hero.hero_overview: Granger is a skill-focused marksman who deals physical damage primarily through abilities. He excels at long-range pressure, quick burst damage, and finishing low-HP enemies, while maintaining mobility through short dashes.
hero.hero_strength: Granger is strong because he can safely deal high physical damage from far away using his skills, move quickly to avoid danger with his dash, and finish low-health enemies easily, making him effective for beginners who focus on good positioning and careful skill use.
hero.hero_weakness: Granger weaknesses are his low survivability dependence on skill accuracy, energy management, and high vulnerability to crowd control, making poor positioning or missed abilities easily punishable. Hard counter is the hero with stronger Crowd control such as Franco / Atlas / Tigreal

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is a great combo for Granger because it allows him to instantly reposition during fights, escape danger, or close the distance to finish low-HP enemies with his skills, effectively maximizing his burst damage while keeping him safe from crowd control or enemy retaliation.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: excels with high-crowd-control teammates and struggles against mobile, burst-heavy assassins.

counters.counter_desc: In 2025, countering Granger requires exploiting his vulnerability during "reload" windows and using items that mitigate his high-burst physical skill damage.

dominants.dominant_desc: Winning with Granger in 2025 revolves around mastering his energy-stacking passive, which scales his cooldowns and damage into the late game.

emblems.emblem_desc: Adaptive damage

equips.equip_desc: In the game Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB), Granger is a marksman hero who can be played with items that lean towards an assassin-style build, focusing on high burst damage and physical penetration rather than consistent attack speed.

hero.hero_overview: a revamped marksman known for high burst damage and mobility.
hero.hero_strength: Granger is a Marksman hero in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang known for his high burst damage, mobility, and unique energy-based mechanics. He does not rely on attack speed and instead converts cooldown reduction into physical attack.
hero.hero_weakness: Granger's main weaknesses in Mobile Legends include his lack of hard Crowd Control (CC), his squishy Marksman nature, and vulnerability to burst damage and high mobility heroes, especially after he uses his dash; focusing on dodging his empowered bullets and punishing him when his skills are on cooldown are key counters. Items like Anti-Curiass (for tanks) and heroes with strong CC or dive potential (Irithel, Ling, Gusion, Saber, Jawhead) effectively exploit these gaps.

data.recommend: To master Granger in 2025, you must adapt to his revamped energy-based playstyle. He is no longer a traditional basic-attack marksman but a high-damage physical mage who scales infinitely by farming.

Tips & Tricks:
🎯 Always position safely and use Death Sonata to finish low-HP targets.
⚡ Use Flicker for repositioning or surprising enemies.
💥 Maximize burst by hitting skills in quick succession.
🏹 Stay behind tanks and avoid crowd control to survive longer.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem setup maximizes Granger's burst damage while giving him small sustain through skill hits and kills,allowing him to stay aggresive and survive despite being squishy.

equips.equip_desc: 📌Granger's build focuses on max burst damage and penetration,letting him quickly eliminate priority targets while relying on positioning for survivals.

hero.hero_overview: Granger is a burst-oriented marksman designed to eliminate priority targets through precise skill execution and disciplined positioning. Unlike traditional attack-speed marksmen, his damage output revolves around timed abilities and ammunition management. Granger excels at mid-to-late game skirmishes, where his ultimate, Death Sonata, allows him to pressure, zone, and decisively finish low-health enemies from long range. However, his effectiveness is heavily dependent on positioning and map awareness, as missteps are severely punished due to limited defensive tools.
hero.hero_strength: Granger excels as a high-impact burst marksman capable of deleting priority targets with precise ability usage. His skill-based damage allows him to remain effective without relying on attack speed, making him strong in early skirmishes and mid-game fights. With Death Sonata, Granger applies immense long-range pressure, enabling him to zone enemies, secure kills, and control objectives from a safe distance. His mobility skill provides repositioning potential, allowing experienced players to maintain optimal spacing during team fights.
hero.hero_weakness: Granger is highly dependent on positioning and precise skill execution; poor spacing or missed abilities significantly reduce his impact in fights. His limited defensive options make him vulnerable to crowd control and burst damage, especially when caught out of position. Additionally, Granger struggles against highly mobile heroes who can quickly close the gap and disrupt his casting rhythm. Without proper map awareness and frontline protection, he can be easily punished in extended engagements.

data.recommend: 📎This guide provides clear strategies for maximizing Granger's burst and synergy.

spell.spell_desc:
🎯 Techniques & Pro Tips
1. Flicker + Death Sonata (Offensive Use)
You can Flicker while casting your Ultimate to instantly adjust your angle.
This catches enemies off-guard and helps land the final shot on fleeing targets.
2. Emergency Repositioning (Defensive Use)
Use Flicker to escape assassins or fighters that dive onto you.
Always Flicker toward safer terrain (turret, teammates, or jungle paths).
3. Gap Closing for Finishes
When an enemy survives with low HP, Flicker forward to secure the kill with your last bullet.
Only do this if key enemy crowd control is already used.
4. Positioning Discipline
Hold Flicker until it is absolutely necessary.
Wasting Flicker early makes Granger extremely punishable.

hero.hero_overview: "Granger" can refer to several things, most commonly Granger, the demon-hunting marksman from Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) known for high burst damage, energy mechanics (Gun & Cannon), and good mobility, making him a strong jungler or gold laner.
hero.hero_strength: Granger's strength in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang lies in his high burst damage, mobility, and unique Demon Energy system, allowing him to deal massive damage with his skills (especially the empowered red bullet and long-range ultimate) and reposition effectively.
hero.hero_weakness: Granger's weaknesses in Mobile Legends include vulnerability to high-mobility assassins and CC (Crowd Control) heroes, difficulty dealing with self-sustaining tanks (like Ruby/Alucard), reliance on precise skill aim (especially his ultimate), and reduced basic attack speed from his passive, making him susceptible to ambush and burst damage if caught out of position. Heroes like Helcurt, Gusion, Selena, Minsitthar, and mobile marksmen like Irithel/Wanwan counter him effectively.

TRY TO NOT GET KILLED, DON'T BE GREEDY IN KILLS, TRY TO LAST HIT GOLD CRAB (if contested by enemy), WAIT FOR YOUR 2 CORE ITEM BEFORE GOING TO FIGHT AND THEN YOU CAN ACHIEVE THIS UNTOUCHABLE PHASE

dominants.dominant_desc: In clash granger should be in the backline and he can span his dash by using ultimate and second skill. When using first skill you can use your ultimate so that the enemy can be burst.

emblems.emblem_desc: This emblem is good for damage and regen

equips.equip_desc: This build is good in burst while rose gold and tought boots gives defense for granger.

hero.hero_overview: Granger needs to stack in early game so in the late game granger can spam his skills.
hero.hero_strength: Granger's power is seen through early, mid, and late game; yes he's power spike is good throughout the game.
hero.hero_weakness: Granger has only one dash which is his second skill so you need to be careful on using his dash to initiate or in retreat

data.recommend: This guide will help you to improve in the game.

spell.spell_desc: Since granger has a dash skill he needs an immune.

data.title: Granger tutorial
